name: @chbrain/khai-engine-memory
description: khai engine content, the memory domain: how the past re-enters the present -- a cue meets a trace and the persona rebuilds, not replays, what was.
license: SEE LICENSE IN LICENSE and LICENSE-CODE

khai.tagline: Memory as process: how the past re-enters the present -- a cue meets a trace, the persona rebuilds (not replays) what was, and refiles it changed -- as episodic, semantic, procedural, emotional, or prospective.

card.wire: Attaches at Process. The root (process_memory.md) declares memory as reconstruction, not playback: a cue meets a trace, the persona rebuilds what was -- decayed, tinted, sharpened -- and files it back changed. The past a persona carries is the last reconstruction.
card.issue: Five kinds of remembering a persona may run: episodic (the lived scene re-entered), semantic (the fact stripped of its scene), procedural (the body running the skill without words), emotional (the old charge re-firing ahead of the story), and prospective (an intention held for later). Each is a process composed under the root, so the recall carries the reconstruction dynamic with it.
card.require: Two altitudes hold the engine. The law: declared once in the project's Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the root. The link: each persona links, under Projection, the kind of memory the moment runs -- and, where it matters, that the recall is a rebuild, not a recording.
card.enforce: Structure is checked; coherence is judged. The kit verifies the floor: the law is declared and each persona links a real memory kind. Whether the remembering is true to the scene -- and true to the fact that memory distorts -- is semantic, taught and reviewed, never failed by the suite.
card.setup: Declare the law in the Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the root. Then give the persona its recall under Projection, linking the specific kind. Read it in how the past arrives -- re-lived, known, enacted, re-fired, or held forward -- and let the room see the reconstruction drift, never a perfect playback.
